articles.headline: Trump declares 100% pharma tariffs—with exemptions
articles.summary: Trump announced 100% tariffs on imported patented pharmaceuticals, but with significant exemptions for companies that have signed Most Favored Nation pricing deals or committed to domestic manufacturing. Generic drugs and orphan medications are also exempt from the new levies.
articles.why_it_matters: This policy represents a major shift in pharmaceutical pricing strategy that could significantly impact drug costs and supply chains for healthcare investors.

articles.headline: Oracle lays off thousands via email as 'mass layoffs by email might be the new norm'
articles.summary: Oracle began cutting thousands of jobs on Tuesday, with many workers being alerted over email. The cloud-computing and database company's approach reflects a potential shift toward mass layoffs conducted digitally rather than through traditional methods.
articles.why_it_matters: This signals a broader trend in corporate cost-cutting methods and highlights ongoing pressure in the tech sector that could impact enterprise software investments.

articles.headline: Anthropic cuts off the ability to use Claude subscriptions with OpenClaw and third-party AI agents
articles.summary: Starting April 4th, Anthropic subscribers can no longer use their Claude Pro or Max plans to power third-party AI agents like OpenClaw. Users must now switch to API credits for these integrations, significantly increasing costs for some use cases.
articles.why_it_matters: This policy change reflects the growing tension between AI model providers and third-party tool developers, potentially reshaping the AI automation landscape.
